name: Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop
name_original: Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda — circular desde Barbadillo del Pez
description: Cycle the difficult 25.6-mile Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop, gaining 1905 feet through forests and past viewpoints in Spain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Barbadillo Del Pez traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys in the Sierra de la Demanda region. The area features a mix of greenways and more challenging mountain paths, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Many routes follow disused railway lines, providing relatively flat sections through scenic countryside. The region's elevation changes contribute to the Barbadillo Del Pez touring bike trails difficulty, with options ranging from moderate to difficult. Best touring cycling routes around Barbadillo Del Pez The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop, a 25.6 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a mix of greenway riding and scenic views through the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra, a difficult 16.2 miles (26.0 km) path. This trail follows a historic railway line, providing a journey through forested areas and open landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Arlanzón – Cantina de Arlanzón loop from Pineda de la Sierra, a 29.9 miles (48.2 km) trail leading through forested hills and rural villages, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Barbadillo Del Pez is defined by greenways, forested valleys, and moderate to difficult ascents. The network offers options for various ability levels, including beginner touring bike routes Barbadillo Del Pez and advanced bike trails Barbadillo Del Pez. The routes in Barbadillo Del Pez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Barbadillo Del Pez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;12 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Barbadillo Del Pez. These routes offer a variety of experiences, ranging from easy to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. While many routes in the area are rated moderate to difficult due to elevation changes, options exist for those new to touring cycling. Look for routes that primarily follow greenways or disused railway lines for a gentler experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: The terrain around Barbadillo Del Pez is diverse, featuring rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys within the Sierra de la Demanda region. You'll find a mix of dedicated greenways, often following disused railway lines for relatively flat sections, and more challenging mountain paths with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: Yes, several circular touring cycling routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027937673/sierra-de-la-demanda-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027950160/via-verde-de-la-demanda-circular-desde-pineda-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; are popular options that bring you back to your starting point.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Barbadillo Del Pez area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31873943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Metal footbridge – Puerto del Manquillo loop from Valle del Sol&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains over 62.6 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón – Cantina de Arlanzón loop from Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, covering 48.2 km through forested hills.

Q: What interesting places or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself. The region also features mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: The touring cycling routes in Barbadillo Del Pez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.8 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 24 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Sierra de la Demanda, and the well-maintained greenways.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: Public transport options directly to the starting points of all routes may be limited in this rural area. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to larger towns like Pineda de la Sierra, which serves as a starting point for several routes. Bringing your own vehicle is often the most flexible option.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages and towns that serve as starting points for the routes, such as Pineda de la Sierra. Look for designated public parking areas. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Barbadillo Del Pez is typically from &lt;strong&gt;late spring to early autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less chance of rain and snow, making for ideal cycling conditions. Summers can be warm, but the forested areas provide shade.

Q: Are there any routes that follow disused railway lines in Barbadillo Del Pez?
A: Yes, the region is known for its greenways that utilize former railway lines. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027950160/via-verde-de-la-demanda-circular-desde-pineda-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a relatively flat and scenic journey through forested areas and open landscapes on a historic railway path.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with visiting mountain peaks in the area?
A: While touring cycling routes typically stick to lower elevations, the Sierra de la Demanda is home to several peaks. You can see prominent summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4002054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mencilla&lt;/a&gt; from various points along the routes. For direct access to the peaks, you would typically switch to hiking or mountain biking.
tips: Wonderfully varied bike tour on an old railway line in the middle of the mountains. Easy-to-drive route, gentle slope, great views - what more could you want.
Panoramic view of Riocavado de la Sierra from the Vía Verde de la Demanda. Riocavado de la Sierra is located at 1,150 meters above sea level on the southern slope of the Port of Manquillo.

This long, spectacular and very photogenic walkway allows passage over the Valdorcas River.
On windy days it adds a little more excitement to the crossing.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty level of the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult for touring bicycles. While sections of the greenway itself feature gentle slopes due to its railway origins, the loop incorporates segments with steeper ascents and descents. Notably, detours around closed tunnels, like the Manquillo tunnel, involve very steep hills that may require pushing your bike. It demands good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The primary surface is compacted gravel, suitable for touring bikes. However, be prepared for unpaved sections, firebreaks, and stream crossings. The route also includes steep detours where original railway tunnels are closed, adding to the challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience wonderfully varied scenery, including dense oak, beech, and pine forests, riparian views along the Arlanzón River, meadows, pastures, and even the edge of a reservoir. Higher points, such as the Manquillo mountain pass, offer scenic views of the surrounding valleys and the Picos de la Demanda.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll cycle along the historic Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda, which follows a former mining railway. Other highlights include a Metal footbridge and a View of Riocavado de la Sierra. The trail also passes through or near charming villages like Pineda de la Sierra, known for its historic-artistic complex.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this greenway loop?
faqs.answer: The humid climate, influenced by the Cantabrian mountain range, means lush vegetation. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, generally spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder conditions and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Barbadillo del Pez. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, you would typically find parking available in or near the villages that serve as access points to the greenway, such as Barbadillo del Pez or Pineda de la Sierra.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The research does not explicitly state regulations regarding dogs on the greenway. It's generally advisable to assume dogs should be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for specific rules.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Demanda Greenway Loop is located within the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda. There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling the greenway itself. However, it's always good practice to respect natural area regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: The region is known for its rich animal biodiversity. You might spot birds of prey, squirrels, foxes, deer, and wild boar. The area is also part of the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Area, which is known to be home to the Iberian wolf.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates significant sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda. It also intersects with other official trails such as the Ruta del Sureste, Etapa 12 Barbadillo de Herreros - Pineda de la Sierra, and Los Seis Azudes, among others.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and the presence of steep detours and unpaved sections, this specific loop is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. While some parts of the greenway are easier, the full loop requires good fitness and some experience with varied terrain.
